summary: Iteration 9 backend-only regression: verified tiny DRY nit in /app/backend/routers/payments.py where admin_edit_payment (L350) now calls the shared _assert_amount_within_outstanding helper instead of inlining the over-amount 400 check. All 3 payment regression suites (test_phase7_payments.py + test_iteration7_refactor_smoke.py + test_iteration8_edit_symmetry.py) = 20/20 passed. Literal 'Amount exceeds outstanding balance' now appears exactly once in payments.py (L249, inside the helper).
critical_code_review_comments: DRY refactor is clean and correct — _assert_amount_within_outstanding is now the single source of the 400 over-amount error message, used in all 3 code paths (admin_record_payment L319, admin_edit_payment L350, and razorpay_create_order over-amount check). Free courses (fee=0) are intentionally exempted inside the helper — same behavior as before.
grep -c confirms exactly 1 occurrence of the literal 'Amount exceeds outstanding balance' remaining in payments.py (L249, inside helper). No duplication.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@rgpacademy.com / Admin@123 (not exercised in this iteration — pytest suites use direct DB/API fixtures)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Payments router iteration_9 = 20/20 green after DRY nit. If additional payment changes come in, extend test_iteration8_edit_symmetry.py or add test_iteration9_*.py. Mocked APIs still in effect: Razorpay env keys empty → 400s expected; Resend in test-mode.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Refactor is a pure DRY simplification and behaviorally identical to iteration_8.
